title: Frontend Interview
description: Build a frontend interview set from focus area, candidate level, and number of rounds.
coachDescription: This set leans toward {{focus}} and follows the depth expected from a {{level}} candidate.

react.0: How would you split component boundaries for a form page so state does not bounce across too many layers?
react.1: When would you choose controlled inputs, and when would uncontrolled inputs be acceptable?
react.2: How do you explain the real risk behind incorrect useEffect dependencies?
react.3: If a list stutters on every keystroke, where would you investigate first?
react.4: How would you reduce breakage when upgrading a shared component library?
react.5: How would you design an accessible modal component for broad reuse?

css.0: When a design shifts heavily across screen sizes, how would you organize responsive rules?
css.1: When do you reach for grid instead of flex?
css.2: How do you avoid style leakage across deep component trees?
css.3: How do you keep mixed text, image, and button layouts from overflowing across languages?
css.4: How would you implement theme color, dark mode, and semantic color tokens?
css.5: How do you shrink the search space when debugging a strange z-index issue?

performance.0: When first paint is slow, how do you separate network, script execution, and rendering bottlenecks?
performance.1: When does lazy loading create real value, and when does it only defer the problem?
performance.2: How would you monitor and explain long-task jank?
performance.3: When is memoization actually worth it in a React page?
performance.4: How do you balance image clarity and loading speed at scale?
performance.5: After shipping a performance optimization, how do you prove it helped?

architecture.0: In a shared frontend repository, how would you divide platform layers from product layers?
architecture.1: What maintenance cost do you see when utility helpers grow without ownership?
architecture.2: What minimum integration standard would you require for a new tool entering the app?
architecture.3: When would you pull logic from a page into ui-kit or a shared runtime?
architecture.4: How do you design a frontend architecture that supports gradual migration without slowing delivery?
architecture.5: How do you tell whether an abstraction helps the team or only adds cognitive load?

junior.0: Can you walk through a simple example?
junior.1: Do you have a bug you hit personally that illustrates this?
junior.2: If you had to fix a page now, what would your first step be?
junior.3: What would the user feel most directly if this went wrong?
junior.4: How would you verify your conclusion?
junior.5: How would you explain this more plainly to a teammate?

mid.0: How would the solution change if the constraints were a little messier?
mid.1: How do you balance maintainability with delivery speed here?
mid.2: If the team had multiple implementations already, how would you drive alignment?
mid.3: Where is this approach most likely to fail?
mid.4: How would you add monitoring or regression protection here?
mid.5: If this became a team standard, what would the most important rule be?

senior.0: If this needed to become platform capability, how would you draw the boundaries?
senior.1: How would you tackle this in a legacy system without a rewrite?
senior.2: What is the key tradeoff in this decision?
senior.3: If team bandwidth were limited, what would you solve first and why?
senior.4: How would you persuade product or management to invest here?
senior.5: From an org perspective, what mechanism really needs to be established behind this problem?
